turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Reporting a Lost Item or Filing a Minor Police Report Scenario: You have lost something important (e.g. your wallet or phone) and need to report it either to a lost-and-found service or to a police station for a minor report. Practise speaking clearly and politely in a formal setting. You will need to define the chatbot’s role as a lost-and-found assistant or police officer. You can choose situations from the examples below; start with a simple report and try a more detailed interaction by asking about recovery steps, documentation, or how to follow up. Learning objectives: • Use past tense and time expressions clearly. • Provide specific object descriptions. • Practise formal and calm reporting language. • (Advanced option) Manage formal reporting interactions by describing the situation clearly, asking about recovery processes, and confirming next steps. Instructions: Try to: • Give clear details about the lost item. • Describe where and when you lost it. • Answer follow-up questions politely. • Ask what happens next and how to follow up. • (Advanced option) Use the extension prompts below to have a longer conversation

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright. I’m sorry to hear you’ve lost something. What happened?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Hi, I've just realized I lost my phone. I'm at the train station, and I'd like to check with the lost and found desk.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, I understand. Can you tell me about when you last had your phone and maybe what it looks like? Let’s start there.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “I have a black phone with a white case, and… I lost it about two hours ago.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it. A black phone with a white case and you think it’s been about two hours since you lost it. Thanks for that info. Now, could you let me know exactly which part of the station you were in when you last remember having it?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “I was in the...”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, I’m with

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Transcript Unavailable

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure, that helps. So you were in the Newcastle station area. Now, can you let me know if you had any identifying details on the phone, like a sticker or any special wallpaper that could help us identify it?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Transcript Unavailable

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, that’s good to know. So you’ve got a black leather wallet with your ID in it as well. Now, just to be sure, could you let me know if there's anything else unique inside that wallet or phone that might help us confirm it’s yours if we find it?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “And the phone has a cracked screen.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure, that’s helpful. So the phone’s got a cracked screen, which makes it easier to identify. Now let me just ask, do you remember if you were near any particular shop or platform in the station when you think you lost it?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Transcript Unavailable

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, that’s a good detail. So you were in the station library area before lunch. Thanks for that. Now, let me ask you, would you like to know the next steps on how we’ll try to recover it and how you can follow up with us?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Yes, sure.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure thing. So what we'll do next is we’ll file a report here, and then you can check back with us in about a day or two to see if it’s turned up. Now, can I just get your contact number or an email so we can reach you if we find it?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Right, before that, what happens if someone find it?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Absolutely. So if someone does find it, we'll contact you using the info you give us, and you can come in and pick it up. Now, could you just share the best phone number or email for us to reach you at?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Okay, here we go.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, got it. Thanks for that. We'll use that info to let you know if we find your phone or your wallet. And if you need anything else, just let us know.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Do I need to show my ID card when collecting?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure, yes, you will. We'll just need to see your ID when you come to pick it up so we can confirm it’s yours. And that’s it. So if you have any more questions, just let me know.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “So, can you call me if it's found?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Absolutely, we can do that. We'll give you a call as soon as we have any news on it. And that should cover it. If there's anything else you need, just let me know.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “So how long do you keep lost items or files?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure, yeah, that's a good question. We typically hold on to lost items for about 30 days. So you’ll have some time to come and pick it up. Anything else you’d like to know?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Should I file an official police report for this?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure, that's totally up to you. For something minor like this, you can if you want a record. But it's not required. Anything else on your mind?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Is there a lost property website I can check?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Absolutely, there is. We do have an online lost property page where you can check for updates. I can give you that link when you come by, or you can find it on our main website. Anything else you’re curious about?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Okay, I appreciate your help.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Of course, happy to help! And if you need anything else, just let us know. Take care!
